name: dough-molding-tool
functional_purpose: mold the dough into a calabash-like shape

construction_steps.reason: the body will help shape the dough

construction_steps.Instruction: Add a wider cylindrical mouth at the top of the body
construction_steps.reason: the mouth will help shape the dough into a calabash-like shape

construction_steps.Instruction: Add a flat circular base at the bottom of the body
construction_steps.reason: the base will provide stability for the tool

construction_steps.Instruction: Add a horizontal cylindrical handle at the top of the tool
construction_steps.reason: the handle will make it easier to hold and maneuver the tool

assemble_func:
def assemble(parts):
    #shape
    wp = cq.Workplane(inPlane=Plane(origin=(0, 0, 0), normal=Vector(0, 1, 0), xDir=Vector(1, 0, 0)))
    shape = wp
    #bool
    result = shape
    #shape
    wp = cq.Workplane(inPlane=Plane(origin=(0, 0, 0), normal=Vector(0, -1, 0), xDir=Vector(1, 0, 0)))
    shape = wp
    #bool
    result_0 = result.union(shape)

    #shape
    wp = cq.Workplane(inPlane=Plane(origin=(0, 0, 0), normal=Vector(0, 1, 0), xDir=Vector(1, 0, 0)))
    shape = wp
    shape = (shape
        .moveTo(0, 0)
        .circle(4)
    )
    #bool
    result_1 = result_0.union(shape)

    #shape
    wp = cq.Workplane(inPlane=Plane(origin=(0, 0, 0), normal=Vector(0, -1, 0), xDir=Vector(1, 0, 0)))
    shape = wp
    shape = (shape
        .moveTo(0, 0)
        .circle(30)
    )
    #bool
    result_2 = result_1.union(shape)

    #shape
    wp = cq.Workplane(inPlane=Plane(origin=(0, 0, 0), normal=Vector(0, 1, 0), xDir=Vector(1, 0, 0)))
    shape = wp
    shape = (shape
        .moveTo(0, 0)
        .circle(30)
    )
    #bool
    result_3 = result_2.union(shape)

    out_path = "assembled_tool.stl"
    cq.exporters.export(result_3, out_path)
    return _ensure_stl_outputs([out_path])


def _ensure_stl_outputs(paths):
    normalized = []
    for p in paths:
        ext = "." + p.rsplit(".", 1)[-1].lower() if "." in p else ""
        if ext == ".stl":
            normalized.append(p)
            continue
        mesh = trimesh.load(p, force='mesh')
        if isinstance(mesh, trimesh.Scene):
            mesh = trimesh.util.concatenate([g for g in mesh.geometry.values()])
        stl_path = (p.rsplit(".", 1)[0] if "." in p else p) + ".stl"
        mesh.export(stl_path)
        normalized.append(stl_path)
    return normalized
